audit_items.proof_trace: The assembly block at L33-37 correctly implements the canonical Solidity bubble-up-revert pattern: `mload(response)` reads the 32-byte length prefix of the `bytes memory` variable (which equals `returndatasize()` post-call), and `add(response, 32)` points to the raw payload. Both guards (`!success` and `response.length > 0`) are present, and no callers bypass this path.
